news.headline: Oak Valley Community Bank hires Jean Turpen as Vice President, Commercial Credit Officer at Roseville Office on Feb 28, 2025.
news.summary: A new addition has been made to Oak Valley Community Bank, a subsidiary of Oak Valley Bancorp (NASDAQ: OVLY), with Jean Turpen taking on the role of Vice President, Commercial Credit Officer. The appointment was confirmed on February 28, 2025, with Turpen being stationed at the Roseville Office situated at 1478 Stone Point Drive in Oakdale, California.

news.headline: Cross Country Healthcare approved acquisition by Aya Healthcare, to be completed in the second half of 2025.
news.summary: Cross Country Healthcare, Inc. (NASDAQ: CCRN) based in Boca Raton, Florida, has received approval from stockholders for its acquisition by Aya Healthcare. The transaction between Cross Country and Aya Healthcare is anticipated to be finalized in the second half of 2025, pending the fulfillment of standard closing conditions outlined in the Merger Agreement.

news.headline: Phoenix Capital Group is now Phoenix Energy, reflecting growth & innovation. Expanded into energy, including mineral rights & operations.
news.summary: On January 27, 2025, in Irvine, California, Phoenix Capital Group Holdings, LLC revealed its new name as Phoenix Energy One, LLC, now known as Phoenix Energy. This shift signifies the company's evolution from a mineral rights acquisition startup to a comprehensive energy enterprise. Phoenix Energy will operate across mineral rights, non-operating working interests, and engaged operations. The rebranding demonstrates the firm's broader focus, technological progress, and dedication to future energy demands.

news.headline: Seeking interest for loan debentures through Finance Innovations. Join us in this opportunity. #Finance #Debentures #Loan
23andMe Special Committee considering strategic alternatives including possible sale of company or assets.
news.summary: On January 28, 2025, in Sunnyvale, California, the Special Committee of the Board of Directors of 23andMe Holding Co. (Nasdaq: ME), a prominent human genetics firm dedicated to enabling individuals to access, comprehend, and leverage the human genome, revealed its initiative to investigate strategic options. These options encompass a potential sale of the company, business merger, asset sale (either wholly or partially), asset licensing, restructuring, or other strategic measures.

news.headline: UXUY's Telegram wallet has 7.8M+ monthly active users & 1.4M+ engaged in Social Trading, becoming the largest in the world.
news.summary: The Telegram wallet (@UXUYbot) of UXUY now has a significant user base, surpassing 7.8 million monthly active users. Among them, 1.4 million users are actively engaged in Social Trading through the platform.

news.headline: Foremost Clean Energy delays Spin-Out closing to January 31, 2025; Shareholders to receive 1 New Foremost Share and 2 Rio Grande Shares per Foremost Share.
news.summary: Foremost Clean Energy Ltd. is spinning out its gold and silver properties into a new company named Rio Grande Resources Ltd. This spin-out is set to occur at 12:01 a.m. on January 31, 2025, one day later than previously announced. Shareholders of Foremost as of January 30, 2025, will receive one new common share of Foremost and two common shares of Rio Grande for each common share of Foremost held. This arrangement aims to separate Foremost's uranium and lithium exploration business from its gold and silver properties, providing shareholders with ownership in both entities.

news.headline: Norsk Hydro issues EUR 500 million green bonds with a 7-year tenor and 3.625% fixed coupon. Successful placement.
news.summary: Norsk Hydro ASA recently completed the issuance of EUR 500 million in senior unsecured green bonds through its Euro Medium Term Note (EMTN) Programme. This newly issued bond has a term of 7 years and features a fixed annual coupon of 3.625 percent (3.649 percent reoffer yield).

news.headline: Mexican airport operator Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico issues Ps. 6.0 billion in bond certificates, oversubscribed 3.4x.
news.summary: In Guadalajara, Mexico, on February 4, 2025, Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico, S.A.B. de C.V. (NYSE:PAC; BMV: GAP) successfully completed the issuance of 60 million long-term bond certificates in Mexico (Certificados Bursátiles) totaling Ps. 6.0 billion. The issuance was split into two tranches, with the option to issue up to Ps. 5.0 billion and a greenshoe option of 20%, which was exercised upon closing. The total order book reached 3.4 times oversubscription over the Ps. 5.0 billion initial offer.

news.headline: Foremost Clean Energy and Rio Grande Resources complete spin-out of Winston Property, merging uranium and lithium exploration companies.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on January 31, 2025, Foremost Clean Energy Ltd. (NASDAQ: FMST) (CSE: FAT) and Rio Grande Resources Ltd. have successfully finalized the spin-out of Foremost's Winston gold and silver properties to Rio Grande. The spin-out, completed through a statutory plan of arrangement under the Business Corporations Act (British Columbia), marks an important milestone for the emerging North American uranium and lithium exploration company and the new stand-alone exploration company. Rio Grande is expected to list on the Canadian Securities Exchange (CSE) following this transaction, which took place this morning as the effective date.

news.headline: TILT Holdings sells 2 MA dispensaries to In Good Health for $2 million to focus on non-plant touching business.
news.summary: On February 03, 2025, TILT Holdings Inc. revealed that it has entered into an Asset Purchase Agreement (APA) to sell two dispensaries in Massachusetts as part of its strategy to optimize operations and enhance value in its vape hardware business, Jupiter Research. TILT, a global cannabis solutions provider, aims to explore strategic alternatives for its plant touching business, focusing on inhalation technologies, cultivation, manufacturing, processing, brand development, and retail services. The company's stocks are listed as Cboe CA:TILT and OTCQB: TLLTF. The divestment aligns with TILT's efforts to consolidate and strengthen its foothold in the cannabis market.

news.headline: H&E Equipment accepts Herc Holdings' offer of $104.89 per share, considered superior to United Rentals' $92.00 offer.
news.summary: H&E Equipment Services, Inc. (NASDAQ:HEES) and Herc Holdings Inc. (NYSE:HRI) are experiencing fluctuations in their shares today. Herc Holdings has made an offer to acquire all outstanding shares of H&E Equipment Services for $104.89 each. This offer is calculated based on Herc's 10-day VWAP and closing price of $200.74 per share on February 14, 2025. The proposal includes $78.75 in cash per share, with a fixed exchange ratio of 0.1287 shares of Herc common stock. The offer, which comprises a fully negotiated merger agreement and financing commitments, will expire on February 24, 2025, at 12:01 p.m. Eastern Time. H&E's Board has deemed Herc's cash and stock merger superior to a previous offer of $92.00 per share from United Rentals, Inc. (NYSE:URI). United Rentals has been informed of H&E's decision.

news.headline: Garrett Motion Inc. reported Q4 sales down 11% to $844M, missing consensus. Gross profit $182M, EPS 47 cents beat 29 cents. Adjusted EBITDA increased to $153M.
news.summary: Garrett Motion Inc. disclosed a decline of 11% in fourth-quarter sales year over year, totaling $844 million, falling short of the expected $860.5 million. The decrease was influenced by reduced diesel production in Europe and weakened gasoline demand in China and North America. Gross profit stood at $182 million, with a margin of 21.6%, surpassing the 20.0% from the prior year. Earnings per share reached 47 cents, beating the projected 29 cents. Adjusted EBITDA rose to $153 million from $145 million due to enhancements in variable and fixed cost productivity, reductions in commodity and transportation costs, energy deflation, and a favorable product mix. Operating cash flow...

news.headline: 84 Lumber celebrates Women in Construction Week 2025, highlighting contributions of women in the industry.
news.summary: Located in Eighty-Four, Pennsylvania, a press release dated February 28, 2025, announces the celebration of Women in Construction (WIC) Week from March 2-8, 2025. 84 Lumber is highlighted for acknowledging women's contributions in the construction sector. As a prominent company in the building materials industry across the nation, 84 Lumber reaffirms its dedication to supporting and recognizing women in construction.

news.headline: Trump administration cancels plan to impose 50% tariff on Colombia.
news.summary: On Sunday, the Trump administration revealed they have decided against implementing a tariff of potentially 50 percent on goods from Colombia.

news.headline: Prime Drink Group Corp. (CSE: PRME) announces strategic overhaul of Prime Bottling to improve profitability and operational efficiency.
news.summary: On February 18, 2025, Prime Drink Group Corp. (CSE: PRME) revealed a strategic restructuring of its subsidiary, Triani Canada Inc. (Prime Bottling). The primary goal of this restructuring is to implement recommendations from an independent financial analysis to evaluate sustainability and enhance profitability at the bottling plant. This move aims to revamp organizational frameworks to boost efficiency in operations and enhance the performance of exclusive brand licenses within the company.

news.headline: CAFI completes FINAL steps with FINRA, to become FIFTY 1 Labs (FITY) in 30 days.
news.summary: In Melbourne, Florida, on February 20, 2025, Consumer Automotive Finance Inc. (OTC: CAFI) shared that it has resolved all last inquiries from the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA) concerning its name and ticker symbol alteration. They are projecting the completion of the shift to Fifty 1 Labs, Inc. (OTC: FITY) in the upcoming 30 days.

news.headline: Beyond to acquire rights to Buy Buy Baby brand, merging it with Bed Bath & Beyond.
Dow Jones initially rose, awaiting Nvidia's earnings report; AI stock surged on data center deal.
news.summary: In the early trading session on Wednesday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced an increase as investors were anticipating Nvidia's significant earnings report in the stock market today. The market was closely watching the performance of Nvidia amidst this anticipation. Additionally, an artificial intelligence stock saw a rise due to a data center deal. The Investor's Business Daily provided live coverage of these developments impacting the stock market today.

news.headline: Ventas will present at Citi 2025 Global Property CEO Conference on March 4 at 1:30 pm. Webcast available on company's website.
news.summary: Ventas, Inc. will have a presentation at the Citi 2025 Global Property CEO Conference on March 4, 2025, at 1:30 p.m. Eastern Time. The presentation will be webcasted on the Company’s website at ir.ventasreit.com/events-and-presentations. The accompanying written materials will also be posted on the Company’s website around March. Ventas, Inc. is listed on NYSE as VTR.

news.headline: Donald Trump uses Emergency Powers Act to impose tariffs on trading partners, raising concerns about executive authority.
news.summary: Trump's decision to utilize the International Emergency Economic Powers Act for imposing broad tariffs on American trading allies demonstrates the expansion of his executive power. This move has sparked controversy and debate, opening up new possibilities in economic policy. The tariffs imposed affect a significant number of trading partners, with implications for a wide range of industries and economic sectors. This decision has highlighted the potential impacts of executive authority on international trade relations, raising concerns among various stakeholders. Trump's actions may set a precedent for future executives and have far-reaching consequences for global trade dynamics.

news.headline: Celanese Corp's shares drop as analysts slash price forecasts post Q4 earnings. 2025 Q1 EPS guidance of 25-50c vs est. $1.63.
news.summary: Shares of Celanese Corporation (NYSE:CE) are experiencing a decline following the release of their fourth-quarter earnings report. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.45, surpassing analysts’ expectations of $1.20, with sales totaling $2.37 billion, aligning with estimates. For the first quarter of 2025, Celanese provided guidance with earnings per share ranging from 25 cents to 50 cents, a significant decrease from the estimated $1.63. Additionally, the company anticipates that its second-quarter earnings per share in 2025 will be approximately $1 higher than in the first quarter. Analyst Jeffrey J. Zekauskas from JPMorgan revised the price forecast from $92 downwards.

news.headline: Walton Isaacson adds Christine Villanueva, Ph.D. & Tony Santabarbara as CFO, promotes 16 staff members. Los Angeles, Feb. 28, 2025 (PRNewswire)
news.summary: Advertising and marketing agency Walton Isaacson (WI) based in Los Angeles revealed major leadership changes today, on February 28, 2025. The agency promoted a total of 16 staff members across various departments as a part of the recent developments. WI is recognized for its forward-thinking and diverse concepts in the industry.

news.headline: Bessent predicts housing market will thaw soon, expects 2% inflation.
Teniz Capital to lead 2nd phase of BSTDB bond placement on AIX after 100M USD 1st tranche in 2024. Aiming to enhance investor participation in Central Asian markets.
news.summary: Teniz Capital Investment Banking will lead the second phase of bond placement for the Black Sea Trade and Development Bank (BSTDB) on the Astana International Exchange (AIX). The first tranche of 100 million USD was completed in 2024. The second tranche aims to support BSTDB’s funding capacity and enhance investor participation in Central Asian markets. The BSTDB, founded in 1999 and based in Greece, has 11 member states. Teniz Capital completed 13 bond transactions in 2023 and has 50 professionals across headquarters in Almaty, Astana, and Abu Dhabi. Last year, the company launched a sister company, Teniz Capital Brokerage Ltd.

news.headline: Trump's tariffs threatening automotive production in North America, causing concern among industry experts.
news.summary: The implementation of President Donald Trump's tariffs poses a severe threat to automotive production throughout North America, according to industry experts and analysts. The potential consequences include a significant slowdown or even a complete stop in production.

news.headline: US stocks set for mixed start after higher inflation data. S&P 500 and Dow down, Nasdaq 100 and Russell 2000 up in premarket. Treasury yields: 10-year at 4.61%, two-year at 4.34%. Fed likely to keep rates unchanged for March meeting. Notable stocks performance on previous day, including Amazon, Alphabet, Tesla, and CVS. S&P 500 ETF mixed, SPY at $603.28, QQQ at $529.07.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures were mixed following a higher-than-expected January inflation report. Pre-market trading showed the S&P 500 and Dow Jones down, while the Nasdaq 100 and Russell 2000 were up. The 10-year Treasury yield was at 4.61%, with a 97.5% chance of the Fed maintaining interest rates at the upcoming March meeting. In the last session, U.S. stocks mostly declined with materials, energy, and real estate sectors leading losses. Key stocks like Amazon and Alphabet fell, while Tesla rebounded and CVS Health surged 15% on strong earnings. The Dow fell 225 points to 44,368.56, the S&P 500 closed down at 6,051.97, the Nasdaq Composite was above the flatline at 19,649.95, and the Russell 2000 fell to 2,255.89.

news.headline: Republicans are unconcerned about inflation, while Democrats are worried.
PPI inflation remains steady with core prices flat, but Airfares halt S&P 500 rally before CPI release.
news.summary: Core prices remained unchanged last month according to the producer price index, resulting in a steady S&P 500 performance leading up to the upcoming release of the consumer price index. Despite concerns regarding Trump's tariffs, the market remained buoyant. The latest data indicates that inflation is under control, with the S&P 500 showing resilience despite fluctuations in the airfare industry. This information was reported by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Will Trump be affected by the transient trap?
Trump plans reciprocal tariffs based on countries' trade policies affecting US, aiming for fairer trade deals globally.
news.summary: The president mentioned that his team will create updated tariff rates based on the trade policies of countries that impact trade with the United States, including tariffs, taxes, subsidies, and other factors.

news.headline: Wall Street reacts calmly to Trump's trade move, is cautious for now.
news.summary: The financial markets on Wall Street have responded positively following President Donald Trump's initiation of trade discussions. Investors have shown confidence in his actions despite initial uncertainties.

news.headline: US producer prices increased to 3.5% in Jan 2025, posing inflation fears, with Fed rate cut hopes diminishing.
news.summary: In January 2025, inflation concerns intensified in the US as prices rose for producers, sparking worries that businesses may either absorb higher costs or transfer them to consumers, potentially hindering Federal Reserve interest rate cuts. The Producer Price Index surged by 3.5% year-over-year, exceeding December’s 3.3% and surpassing the 3.2% forecast. This spike marked the highest annual reading since February 2023 and the fourth consecutive monthly increase, indicating persistent inflationary pressures. On a monthly basis, the PPI increased by 0.4%, down from December’s 0.5% but exceeding the projected 0.3%. This unexpected rise in the PPI follows a strong Consumer Price Index report.

news.headline: Republicans in Congress are focusing on delivering policy wins for the cryptocurrency industry after promising for years.
news.summary: For a significant amount of time, Republican lawmakers in the Capitol have pledged to provide the cryptocurrency sector with a comprehensive set of policy victories.

news.headline: Fed chief Powell highlights "policy uncertainty" under Trump; Investor's Business Daily discusses four rate outlook scenarios.
news.summary: Jerome Powell, the Federal Reserve chairman, expressed concerns about "policy uncertainty" during Donald Trump's administration, which is affecting the Federal Reserve rate outlook. This statement underscores the challenges faced by the Fed in setting interest rates amidst the changing economic landscape. The impact of this policy uncertainty on the economy and the Fed's decisions is significant, especially as there are four possible scenarios that could unfold, each with its implications for the future. Powell's remarks highlight the complexity of the current economic situation and the need for careful consideration in determining the appropriate course of action.

news.headline: Uncertainty in tariffs & policies may harm businesses by hindering hiring and investing. Strong US economy offers some protection.
news.summary: Uncertainty surrounding tariffs and other policies may lead to a negative impact on hiring and investment. However, the robust U.S. economy is expected to serve as a protective measure against these potential downturns.

news.headline: Banks anticipated an easier time under Trump's presidency with a Republican Congress, but are still waiting for changes.
news.summary: With hopes for a smoother road ahead, banks were optimistic about the possibility of eased operations with President Donald Trump elected alongside a Republican Congress.

news.headline: US and Russia start peace talks on Ukraine with first high-level meeting. Markets steady, FDA prepares to release FOMC minutes.
news.summary: In midday trading on Tuesday, major U.S. indices showed minimal changes while investors kept an eye on geopolitical updates and awaited the release of the latest FOMC minutes. U.S. and Russian officials initiated peace discussions in Saudi Arabia regarding the Ukraine war, marking their first high-level talks since Russia's 2022 invasion. These talks aimed to build trust and potentially set the stage for a future meeting between President Trump and President Putin, with the absence of Ukrainian and European officials being notable. WTI crude oil futures remained stable as U.S.-Russia diplomatic talks hinted at decreased geopolitical tensions. The yield on the 10-year US Treasury note saw a 0.04% increase, surpassing the 4.5% mark amid investor reactions. For more details, visit Benzinga.com for the full story.

news.headline: Fed halts rate cuts due to strong labor market and inflation; S&P 500 falls amid policy uncertainty.
news.summary: The Federal Reserve decided to halt rate cuts due to ongoing inflation rates, a stable labor market, and uncertain policy factors. This decision came as President Trump introduced new policies. Additionally, the S&P 500 experienced a decline in response to this news.

news.headline: Union push at North Carolina warehouse fails; workers miss second election win at Amazon facility, vows to keep organizing.
news.summary: The recent result marked a setback for workers attempting to secure a repeat victory in a second election at an Amazon facility. Despite this, the union declared its commitment to persist in their efforts to organize.

news.headline: Federal Reserve concerns over handling price hikes from Trump's tariffs. Officials assess impact on economy.
news.summary: Government leaders are struggling to determine the best course of action in dealing with possible price hikes resulting from the policies put in place by the administration.

news.headline: Immigrant surge under Biden helped labor market, deportations could halt flow of new workers.
news.summary: During Joe Biden's administration, a significant increase in immigrants entering the labor market has played a crucial role in suppressing inflation. Conversely, under Donald Trump, the number of new workers entering the market may decrease significantly, potentially leading to a slowdown in the labor force.

news.headline: Fed reports show inflation rate remains high, with positive readings for two months. S&P 500 futures increase.
news.summary: The inflation rate monitored by the Federal Reserve remains high, maintaining a positive trend for the second consecutive month. Concurrently, S&P 500 futures experienced an increase. This comes as key data regarding inflation and wages from the Federal Reserve alleviated concerns prior to the imposition of tariffs. The S&P 500 index is on track to potentially set a new record amid these developments.

news.headline: Trump's tariffs could hinder growth as critics warn of price surges due to new tariffs.
news.summary: Critics have raised concerns about President Donald Trump's trade agenda, stating that the implementation of new tariffs could lead to a significant increase in prices.

news.headline: Hawkish jobs report and increased inflation expectations reduce Fed rate-cut odds; S&P 500 falls.
news.summary: An increase in inflation expectations and a moderately hawkish jobs report have negatively impacted the likelihood of a rate cut by the Federal Reserve. As a result, the S&P 500 index experienced a decline following these developments. The report highlighted growing concerns over the potential for a rate cut, indicating a shift in market sentiment.

news.headline: A.I. is revolutionizing Silicon Valley start-ups - fewer workers, more productivity, success with small teams.
news.summary: In the past, tech start-ups would often secure significant funding to recruit large numbers of employees and scale rapidly. However, the emergence of artificial intelligence technologies has led to increased worker efficiency, resulting in a rise of success stories centered around "tiny teams" with fewer workers. This shift signals a change in the traditional approach of focusing on acquiring substantial funds for hiring purposes.

news.headline: Trump mulls ending all ongoing military aid to Ukraine - WaPo.
Mike Lindell and his team at CPAC along with TV personalities. They hit the ground running with interviews and news.
news.summary: Upon arriving at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC), Mike Lindell and his media team wasted no time in getting started. They swiftly began conducting interviews and reporting on the latest news from the event. Lindell and his team are fully immersed in covering CPAC, ensuring that their audience stays informed and engaged.

news.headline: President plans to increase taxes on private equity profits to fund campaign promises, such as eliminating taxes on overtime, tipped income, and Social Security benefits.
news.summary: The administration is considering raising taxes on private equity gains in order to fund key campaign pledges, including removing taxes on overtime, tipped earnings, and Social Security benefits. This move aims to address fiscal demands as part of the populist agenda, with an increase in tax rates on private equity profits being proposed by the president. This action is projected to bring in substantial revenue to support these initiatives, highlighting a strategic approach to financing key policy changes.

news.headline: Trump's spending freeze creates chaos as tens of thousands of projects face possible risk, alarming supporters.
news.summary: Advocates for various initiatives in climate, infrastructure, mortgages, technology, healthcare, veterans' affairs, and other sectors are raising concerns as over tens of thousands of projects are potentially in jeopardy.
